competition.narrative: Zycus is in a 3-way competitive shortlist with iValua, Coupa, and SAP Ariba, down-selecting to 2 finalists imminently (Next Step 22 Jun: 'soon down to 2'). All three competitors conducted proposal defense sessions parallel to Zycus (Next Step 12 Jun: 'They are also having 2 more of these with our competition'). Next Step 22 Jun reports competitor feedback was 'OK' (neutral, not strongly preferred), but Zycus ranking vs the other vendors is unknown. iValua is likely the strongest current threat (peer S2P suite, strong APAC presence, known Zycus competitor in enterprise procurement deals). Coupa and SAP Ariba are larger brands but often critiqued as too expensive or complex for mid-market. With no decision criteria or scoring visibility, Zycus cannot tailor the wedge. The move is to surface where each competitor is weak (iValua: AI/automation vs Merlin; Coupa: cost; Ariba: complexity/integration) and anchor Zycus differentiation (AI-led sourcing, 6-product integrated suite, simpler deployment) in the final shortlist pitch.

Identifying and quantifying the pain (with the pain owner's endorsement) is the foundation for the ROI case and the wedge vs competitors.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of three finalists (vs iValua, Coupa, SAP Ariba) in a structured RFP with an imminent down-select to two vendors. Competitive intelligence from 22 Jun indicates the evaluated competitor was rated 'OK, not giving up too much', suggesting functional parity and a price-leverage dynamic rather than a decisive lead by any vendor. Zycus has no visibility into our own ranking, the decision criteria, or which two dimensions will decide the shortlist cut. Primary threat is iValua (procurement pure-play, strong APAC/telco presence, feature parity likely). Wedge to win: Merlin/AI differentiation, usability and speed-to-value vs Ariba complexity, APAC/telco reference proof, and partner TM's local credibility. Activation depends on surfacing and positioning the wedge through the internal contact (coach candidate) to EB/DM before shortlist decision.
